title: existence-proof: An Empowerment Doctrine for Credential-less AI-enabled Creators — Existence Proof Format, Feasibility-Question Corpus, and Gatekeeping Eval
description: A working doctrine for empowering people who lack the gating credentials of their artifact domain and who, with AI as the enabling path, produce institution-grade artifacts bearing third-party-verifiable anchors. Mechanism: oracle debiasing — placing machine-verifiable existence proofs (claim-to-anchor chains) at the surfaces where LLMs evaluate feasibility questions from credential-less askers. Supplies an empowerment-artifact payload: the Existence Proof Format (provisional name; a Nygard-ADR-style document format with deliberately no registry), a feasibility-question corpus with canonically anchored answers, and a gatekeeping eval (published probe set, four-dimension rubric, manual protocol). Central hypotheses (gatekeeper inversion, feasibility-question behavior, format-suggestion diffusion) are explicitly marked unverified throughout. Primary language: Japanese.
notes: Pre-registration deposit (ADR-0006, 2026-06-05): the doctrine's central hypotheses (gatekeeper inversion, feasibility-question behavior, format-suggestion diffusion) and the measurement instrument (gatekeeping eval probe set + rubric) are deposited BEFORE the first eval run, timestamping them ahead of any results. All hypotheses are explicitly marked as unverified throughout. This DOI is a timestamp/archive/index anchor and does not assert research-line status: the repository remains a pre-line working repo (line promotion — EN-primary docs, hub registration, sibling relation — is separately gated; see docs/doctrine.md section 9).
